page: Microsoft Azure D16as_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DASv4 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 16 dedicated virtual CPUs, mapped from 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, alongside support for AVX, AES, VAES, and SHA-NI instruction sets.

This server includes 131 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance disk options. It does not include any bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests and a strong PassMark memory score, placing both in the top 25% tier. Most other performance metrics, including multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, software compilation, and database operations, fall within the average middle 50% range. The server is suitable for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, software development, database operations, and moderate-scale text or image processing.
description: Microsoft Azure D16as_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DASv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance offers 16 dedicated vCPUs derived from 8 physical cores, paired with 64.0 GB of system memory. The processor features 32 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AES, and SHA-NI. It includes 131 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, though it lacks dedicated GPU hardware. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities and strong memory scores, while multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform within the average tier. This resource profile makes the D16as_v4 highly suitable for balanced workloads such as web serving, application hosting, software compilation, and relational database management.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16as_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and 131 GB of local storage, delivering strong single-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16as_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 131 GB local storage, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, premium storage capabilities, and strong single-core performance.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 64 GB of system memory and 131 GB of local storage
Supports Premium Storage for high-performance disk configurations
Delivers strong single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
Well-suited for web serving, software compilation, and database operations
